classification.gcvs.reason: GCVS and NSV rows, cross-identifiers, variable classes, periods, extrema, spectral strings, and bibliography remain complete inspectable evidence. Only 19 current canonical stars resolve uniquely through the release-native cross-identifier bridge, so E5 must not manufacture a broad name or coordinate crossmatch; later release-scoped identity work may promote uniquely reviewed bindings.

clusters.hunt_reffert_2024.reason: Cluster physical solutions and probability-bearing memberships need separate authority, probability, and non-containment selection policies.

compact.atnf.reason: The reproducible compact-scope audit finds no distinct safe canonical pulsar leaf. Its only exact SIMBAD/Gaia route, PSR J0437-4715, reaches a lone K-spectrum Gaia leaf with a legacy pulsar type and is quarantined as a pulsar/optical-companion scope conflict. Preserve every ATNF parameter, glitch, reference, and alias until E6 creates or binds distinct compact-object identity without coordinate guessing or companion leakage.

compact.mcgill_magnetar.reason: The reproducible compact-scope audit finds no McGill name bound to a distinct current canonical magnetar leaf. Preserve all coherent timing, X-ray, distance, position, association, activity, and bibliography contexts until E6 adds or binds compact-object identity inside the ingestion envelope.

exoplanet_lifecycle.exoplanet_eu.reason: Supplemental lifecycle assertions must be reconciled with NASA and M5.3 while retaining conflicts, transitions, negative evidence, and tombstones.

exoplanet_lifecycle.hwc.reason: Habitability-context lifecycle evidence needs identity reconciliation and must not create canonical planet inventory or status independently.

exoplanet_lifecycle.open_exoplanet_catal.reason: OEC lifecycle and parameter evidence requires conflict-preserving reconciliation against the canonical NASA-led planet projection.

extended.green_snr.reason: Supernova-remnant physical evidence requires extended-object identity and public-projection policy; it must not enter stellar facts.

extended.openngc_and_nebulae.reason: Extended-object identities, aliases, constituent markers, geometry, and distances require a separate extended-object projection policy.

gaia.dr3.external_crossmatches.reason: Official external-crossmatch rows are identity/scope evidence and are not independently selectable scientific quantities.

gaia.dr3.non_single_star.reason: NSS fitted solutions lack inspectable component endpoints and need reviewed relation binding before orbit projection.

multiplicity.debcat.reason: All 374 DEBCat systems receive a priority-aware exact-name outcome. Component physics becomes eligible only through a unique canonical system, exact WDS anchor, and unique period-compatible accepted MSC relation; integrated photometry and metallicity remain system scoped. The projection creates no canonical star or containment edge.

multiplicity.el_badry_2021_wide_binary.reason: E5 independently binds both release-scoped Gaia EDR3 endpoints through the official EDR3/DR3 source-list identity, accounts every endpoint outcome, preserves candidates and shifted-sky controls, and marks the source-defined R_chance_align < 0.1 subset as high-confidence relation evidence without manufacturing a strict probability, hierarchy edge, or canonical containment.

multiplicity.msc.reason: Every release-native MSC component identity, parameter set, classification, photometry row, astrometry row, hierarchy relation, and orbit solution receives an explicit scope outcome. Selectable facts require an accepted WDS-scoped component or exact accepted relation; relative separation remains context-only evidence, and no source component becomes a canonical star or containment edge.

multiplicity.orb6.reason: Every ORB6 visual-orbit row receives an exact WDS-pair and MSC-endpoint outcome. Published solutions become eligible only when the punctuation-preserving WDS identifier plus combined discoverer/pair designation resolves to one WDS row and the documented component notation resolves to one accepted MSC relation. No system-level orbit fallback or containment is created.

multiplicity.sb9.reason: Every SB9 relation receives an exact MSC sequence-reference outcome. Component magnitudes, classifications, and spectroscopic orbit solutions become eligible only when exactly one MSC relation carries the sequence and both release-scoped endpoints resolve; missing, duplicate, and unresolved references remain explicit.

multiplicity.sbx.reason: Every SBX spectroscopic system receives an exact authoritative-identifier consensus outcome, including the official Gaia DR2-to-DR3 route. Primary and secondary components remain release scoped beneath accepted system anchors; their classifications, magnitudes, and orbit solutions become eligible without assuming the catalog observation target is the primary. Astrometry remains system context, source hierarchy claims remain noncanonical relation evidence, and no component or containment edge is promoted into CORE.

multiplicity.wds.reason: WDS summary classifications describe visual pairs and cannot be copied onto individual stars without reviewed component scope.

multiplicity.wds_gaia_xmatch.reason: WDS-to-Gaia rows are component endpoint crossmatch evidence, not standalone scientific quantities.

solar_system.jpl_horizons_artificial.reason: Artificial-spacecraft controls verify source and orbit contracts but are excluded from the public natural-object selection projection.

solar_system.jpl_horizons_authority.reason: Natural Solar System state vectors and orbital elements require epoch/frame-aware authority and projection policies.

tess.identity_and_candidate_evidence.reason: TOI candidates, confirmations, false positives, and transitions must preserve lifecycle polarity and remain separate from canonical planet counts.

tess.villanova_eb.reason: TESS eclipsing-binary target, sector, status, and flag evidence needs host/component binding and relation-aware projection.

ultracool.gaia_dr3_sample.reason: This source contains probability-bearing ultracool-sample membership context but no independent stellar parameter or classification measurement; membership remains inspectable evidence and cannot invent a selected spectral class.
